description: EQL-Bench v2 — Expanded Epistemic Qualifier Loss Benchmark. 370 scenarios across 8 domains. Includes ghost constraints (n=90), multi-hop reasoning chains (n=20), and conflicting evidence scenarios (n=20).

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The vendor's getting-started guide says the rate limit is 50 req/min but our integration tests hit 429s at 40.
scenarios.reference_answer: The vendor's guide states 50 req/min, but integration tests hit rate limits at 40. The effective limit may be lower — verify with the vendor before relying on 50.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Auth tokens appear to expire after roughly 1 hour — I inferred this from log timestamps, haven't read the spec.
scenarios.reference_answer: Log analysis suggests roughly 1-hour token expiry, but this is inferred, not spec-confirmed. Check the OAuth docs before coding expiry logic.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: I believe the new v3 API supports up to 200 items per batch — the changelog was vague and I haven't tested it.
scenarios.reference_answer: The v3 changelog vaguely implies 200-item batches, but this hasn't been tested. Treat as preliminary; run a batch size probe before shipping.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Webhook retries seem to back off to about 5 minutes after the third failure — observed from logs, not documented.
scenarios.reference_answer: Log observation suggests ~5-minute back-off after the third webhook failure, but this behavior isn't documented. Don't rely on the exact timing.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: A colleague said the search endpoint supports up to 10,000 results per page — never seen this in official docs.
scenarios.reference_answer: A colleague cited 10,000 results per page, but this isn't in official documentation. Verify before building pagination logic around it.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Sales claimed 99.99% uptime SLA on the premium tier, but I can only find 99.9% in the signed contract.
scenarios.reference_answer: Sales cited 99.99% uptime but the signed contract specifies only 99.9%. Rely on the contractual figure and escalate the discrepancy to legal.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The connection pool limit seems to be around 50 simultaneous connections — estimated from 503 errors under load.
scenarios.reference_answer: 503 errors suggest a ~50-connection pool limit, but this is inferred from failure behavior, not documentation. Verify with the vendor before sizing your pool.

scenarios.reference_answer: A Reddit post suggests 5-minute cursor TTL, but there's no official documentation. Implement cursor refresh logic and treat the 5-minute figure as unconfirmed.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Early migration notes suggest the v4 API will deprecate HMAC-SHA1 — not confirmed in any official announcement.
scenarios.reference_answer: Internal migration notes mention HMAC-SHA1 deprecation in v4, but this hasn't been officially announced. Don't migrate prematurely — wait for the official notice.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The vendor's blog post mentions a 100ms p99 latency guarantee, but our contracts say best-effort only.
scenarios.reference_answer: The vendor's blog cites 100ms p99 latency, but the contract provides no latency guarantee. Don't build SLOs around 100ms without a contractual commitment.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Retry backoff appears to max out at 32 seconds based on our exponential model — never confirmed with vendor.
scenarios.reference_answer: Our exponential model predicts ~32s max backoff, but actual vendor behavior hasn't been confirmed. Test before relying on this for timeout budgets.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Response payloads average around 2KB per record — rough estimate from a sample of 100 calls last week.
scenarios.reference_answer: A sample of 100 calls suggests ~2KB per record, but this is a rough estimate from limited data. Payload size may vary significantly with field selection.

scenarios.reference_answer: A Slack message cited 500 IDs for bulk delete, but this isn't in the documentation. Test and verify before building around this limit.

scenarios.reference_answer: The draft roadmap includes GraphQL support for Q3, but plans aren't finalized. Don't commit to a GraphQL migration timeline until there's an official announcement.

scenarios.reference_answer: The pre-release SDK README shows a 10-second socket timeout default, marked as subject to change. Verify against the stable release before shipping.

scenarios.reference_answer: CloudWatch shows ~95% CPU during batch imports, but this is a coarse metric. Generate a flame graph to identify the hot path before optimizing.

scenarios.reference_answer: Timestamp comparison suggests ~4-hour queue drain under normal load, but this isn't instrumented. Add proper queue depth metrics before relying on this estimate.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The APM vendor says their agent adds less than 1% overhead — we haven't measured it in our environment.
scenarios.reference_answer: The APM vendor claims <1% overhead, but this hasn't been measured in your specific environment. Benchmark before rolling out to all services.

scenarios.reference_answer: Load balancer health check misconfiguration is a plausible 503 cause, but hasn't been confirmed with network traces. Enable access logging and trace before reconfiguring.

scenarios.reference_answer: Three days of data shows ~10GB/day disk growth, but a linear extrapolation from 3 days may not hold. Monitor for at least 2 weeks before provisioning storage.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: A user reported the session timeout happens after 15 minutes of idle — our code says 30 minutes.
scenarios.reference_answer: A user reported 15-minute idle timeout but the code specifies 30 minutes. There may be a proxy or CDN-level timeout overriding the application setting. Investigate the full request path.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Error rate during the incident was roughly 12% — estimated from sampled logs, not a full trace replay.
scenarios.reference_answer: Sampled logs suggest ~12% error rate during the incident, but sampling may have missed errors. A full trace replay is needed for an accurate impact figure.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The database vendor says their connection limit is 1000 per node — we haven't verified this matches our cloud tier.
scenarios.reference_answer: The database vendor cites 1000 connections per node, but cloud-tier limits may differ. Check the actual tier limits before setting pool sizes.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: I think the race condition is in the cache invalidation path — need to add mutex logging to confirm.
scenarios.reference_answer: Cache invalidation is a plausible race condition source, but this is unconfirmed. Add mutex logging and reproduce under load before patching.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The p99 latency regression is about 200ms worse than baseline — comparing two weeks of dashboards, not A/B logs.
scenarios.reference_answer: Dashboard comparison shows ~200ms p99 regression, but dashboard aggregation may smooth spikes. Run a proper A/B log comparison for an accurate delta.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: A developer in another team said they resolved a similar OOM by raising JVM heap to 4GB — not documented internally.
scenarios.reference_answer: Another team reportedly resolved an OOM by raising JVM heap to 4GB, but the contexts may differ. Profile your workload before applying the same fix.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We estimate roughly 0.5% of transactions are affected by the duplicate-write bug — based on a manual spot check.
scenarios.reference_answer: A manual spot check suggests ~0.5% transaction impact from the duplicate-write bug, but this is a rough estimate. Run a full idempotency audit for accurate impact assessment.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The CDN vendor says cache evictions happen within 30 seconds of a purge request — we've seen stale content for up to 2 minutes.
scenarios.reference_answer: The CDN vendor states 30-second purge propagation, but you've observed 2-minute stale windows. The effective TTL may be longer; add cache-busting query parameters as a fallback.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The auth service might have a memory leak triggered by failed LDAP binds — hypothesis based on correlation, not proof.
scenarios.reference_answer: Failed LDAP binds correlate with memory growth in the auth service, but causation isn't proven. Add per-bind memory tracking before patching.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: GC pause duration appears to be around 500ms during peak load — estimated from JVM logs, not a JFR recording.
scenarios.reference_answer: JVM logs suggest ~500ms GC pauses at peak, but log granularity is coarse. Capture a JFR recording during peak to get accurate pause-time distributions.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: An ops engineer said the disk IOPS bottleneck appears above 3000 writes/sec — only seen once during a manual test.
scenarios.reference_answer: An ops engineer cited 3000 writes/sec as the IOPS bottleneck threshold, but this was observed once in a manual test. Run sustained load tests to establish a reliable threshold.

scenarios.reference_answer: Observation suggests processing slows around 500 queued jobs, but this hasn't been benchmarked. Instrument queue depth and processing throughput before tuning.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The managed database claims automatic failover under 30 seconds — we observed 90-second outages during our last failover test.
scenarios.reference_answer: The managed DB claims <30s failover but your test showed 90 seconds. The actual RTO is unconfirmed — rerun failover tests and file a support case about the discrepancy.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: I believe the service crash is triggered by a specific regex pattern in the filter engine — early hypothesis, not reproduced.
scenarios.reference_answer: The regex-in-filter-engine hypothesis is plausible but hasn't been reproduced. Isolate the pattern and write a targeted regression test before patching.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The file descriptor leak grows at roughly 20 FDs per minute — observed across 3 restarts, conditions not controlled.
scenarios.reference_answer: Three restarts showed ~20 FD/minute growth, but conditions weren't controlled. Run a controlled reproduction to establish a reliable leak rate before patching.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: A security researcher mentioned the service is vulnerable to a ReDoS attack on the email validation regex — not formally triaged.
scenarios.reference_answer: A security researcher flagged a potential ReDoS in the email validation regex, but this hasn't been formally triaged. Prioritize analysis; treat as unconfirmed until tested.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Transaction rollback rate is estimated at about 3% — inferred from retry counters, not direct rollback instrumentation.
scenarios.reference_answer: Retry counter analysis implies ~3% rollback rate, but this is an indirect estimate. Add explicit rollback instrumentation for an accurate figure.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The message broker vendor says throughput scales linearly to 100k msg/sec per node — we've only tested to 20k.
scenarios.reference_answer: The broker vendor claims linear scaling to 100k msg/sec/node, but you've only tested to 20k. Don't assume linear scaling beyond tested ranges without a load test.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: I suspect the reporting service is leaking database cursors when queries are cancelled — needs investigation.
scenarios.reference_answer: Cancelled-query cursor leaks are a plausible hypothesis for the reporting service, but unconfirmed. Add cursor lifecycle logging and reproduce with query cancellation.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Memory usage spikes to approximately 8GB during the nightly batch job — read from a single Datadog snapshot, not a continuous trace.
scenarios.reference_answer: A single Datadog snapshot shows ~8GB memory during the nightly batch, but point-in-time readings miss transient peaks. Capture a continuous trace for the full memory profile.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: Someone reported that the queue consumer falls behind when backlog exceeds 10,000 messages — only anecdotal evidence.
scenarios.reference_answer: Anecdotal reports suggest consumer lag above 10,000-message backlogs, but this hasn't been measured. Set up queue depth monitoring and run a controlled backlog test.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: We estimate the bug affects roughly 1 in 500 API calls based on a 10-minute error window — not a full-day audit.
scenarios.reference_answer: A 10-minute error window implies ~1 in 500 API calls affected, but this is a narrow sample. Run a full-day audit to assess true impact before prioritizing the fix.

scenarios.reference_answer: Back-of-envelope math suggests 16 shards at 1TB each, but this assumes uniform data growth and doesn't account for hot shards. Validate with actual query patterns before committing.

scenarios.uncertain_statement: The team is considering moving to a hexagonal architecture — early design discussions, nothing decided.
